rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDDIE MAE LUCKETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court will determine on continuance. The following issues are still pending: Procedural Issues on PETITION: (1) Notice of hearing must be given to all persons entitled. (2) Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance and Access must be given, too. LRSF 14.1.C. (3) Proposed order must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A.1). - The proposed order lodged in Feb merely addresses the objection. No order for the petition. (4) Petition caption alleges waiver of account; however, no written waiver of account is on file. Depending on the response to the above issue, the petition needs to clarify whether all eligible distributees' waivers of account are on file or not. In the absence of an account, a new or amended final account petition seems appropriate. (5) Petition to propose allocation of statutory compensation to the attorneys for the personal rep. PrC. 10814. (6) Need explanation on request for reimbursement for 6/25/2025 special appearance counsel-court hearing for $281.75. There was no hearing requiring an appearance. (7) Need an explanation of the TBD requests for reimbursement of delivery costs. If the funds have been paid, an update seems necessary. Procedural Issues on OBJECTION: (1) court's authorization to distribute. It appears that a fully noticed petition, with proper notice of hearing (PrC 11604(d)), may be necessary. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HURBERT LEE DONALDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) The allegation on the petition seems contradictory as to whether the decedent died testate or intestate. (2) The examiner is unable to complete the review because this petition is missing information; not all sections have been filled out. (3) Additionally, other required documents, such as the original will, notice, proposed order, and duties and liabilities, are not on file. The Court will decide whether to dismiss this petition. (4) The supplement filed by the petitioner on 3/3/2026 does not support the request to admit the decedent's will to probate or to appoint the petitioner as the personal representative of the estate. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT FUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/2026. The following issues to be cleared before 4/3/2026. (1) Proof of publication not on file. (2) Petitioner's verified supplement to identify the trustee and the beneficiaries of the sole devisee trust in #8 of the petition. If the petitioner is the sole trustee, all beneficiaries of the trust must be identified and noticed (3) Petition #8 to indicate the date of death of the deceased spouse. (4) Waiver of bond from Mia Fung as the decedent's will seems to waive bond for Marco and Mia to act as co-executors.
